characters.physical_details: Brown-haired Hufflepuff with ink-stained fingers and a branded right palm; by the battle he has cracked nails and recurrent nosebleeds.

key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Closest early friend; friendship ends after his repeated refusals to spend the work on her.
key_relationships.prue_hollis: Manuscript collaborator and final witness to his battle-night refusal.
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: Resistance organizer who first dismisses him, then bars him from operations.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Headmaster who recognizes amendment traces and warns him about the castle.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Immediate bodily threat and repeated trigger for his failed interventions.
key_relationships.tallow: Kitchen elf guide and later node-maintenance counterpart.

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year, secret First Art researcher, and by dawn after the battle the node's keeper.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English, school spell Latin, and basic runes; by spring can parse Meridian maintenance notation.

state_transitions.change: Gets the coffer brand and takes the Grinlow Leaves.
First amendment destabilizes his ordinary wandwork around thresholds and closures.
Learns that a major battle at Hogwarts will whiten the breach and attract outside predatory attention.
Repairs the seam, discovers the hostile-wandwork clause, refuses it, and takes severe physical strain.
Signs himself to the node and begins first-light/last-light maintenance after the Leaves are absorbed.

characters.availability: Present all year; below the castle during the battle's decisive stretch; alive afterward.

characters.physical_details: Compact and broad-shouldered, usually in a dark plait; by late winter she carries split knuckles, a left-leg limp, and later a swollen knee and strapped right hand.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Closest early friend, then the person she sees choose the deeper work over immediate human need.
key_relationships.prue_hollis: Uneasy ally linked by worry and later shared judgment of Owen.
key_relationships.tallow: Kitchen helper who feeds and patches her.

characters.occupation_status: Hufflepuff fifth-year, resistance courier, and caregiver in crises.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English and ordinary Hogwarts texts; no First Art literacy.

state_transitions.change: Great Hall punishment leaves visible injuries and shows her Owen can distort immediate magical violence.
Lower-west sabotage fails; Amycus makes her the corridor example.
Detention worsens her knee and hand; she sees Owen keep a prepared page for the lower door and ends the friendship.
Stays in the fight and triage network despite the limp.

characters.availability: Present through battle and aftermath; alive afterward, but no longer emotionally available to Owen.

characters.physical_details: Tall Ravenclaw with dark curls, wire-rimmed spectacles, and ink-black cuffs.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Bibliographic ally who becomes the direct witness of his refusal.
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Practical ally linked by concern over Owen.
key_relationships.tallow: Mutual tolerance grounded in books and kitchens.

characters.occupation_status: Ravenclaw sixth-year, archive-minded astronomy helper and Owen's main collaborator.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads English fluently; strong on bibliography, astronomy overlays, runic annotation, and enough Meridian and founder notation to map the anchor tables.

state_transitions.change: Recognizes Owen's citation as impossible in normal printings.
Proves the citation survived in an early print lineage and becomes a full collaborator.
Maps the four anchors and realizes Owen may not choose people over repair.
Offers the 'help the living first' compromise and loses trust when he cannot promise it.
Reaches the chamber, sees the live clause, and witnesses his refusal.

characters.availability: Present through battle and dawn; alive afterward; one of very few people who knows what Owen chose below.

characters.physical_details: Solidly built, often bruised, and visibly worn by command labor.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Student he first dismisses, then bars from operations and planning.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Headmaster he opposes as part of the regime.

characters.occupation_status: Gryffindor student and de facto organizer of the in-school resistance until the open battle.
characters.literacy_languages: Standard Hogwarts spell literacy; no direct First Art knowledge.

state_transitions.change: Rejects Owen's manuscript warning as unusable in wartime conditions.
Bars Owen from further resistance jobs after the corridor fiasco.
Keeps Owen off routes, meetings, and message work after the Snape summons.
Becomes open battle commander when Harry returns.

characters.availability: Present all year and central to the surface battle; alive afterward.

characters.physical_details: Sallow, black-haired, severe, and visibly exhausted.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Student under watch because amendment traces keep scarring the school.
key_relationships.amycus_carrow: Nominal ally whose ignorance of old ward damage he preserves.

characters.occupation_status: Headmaster under Voldemort's regime, publicly complicit and privately running a narrower game.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in magical theory and older scholarship around amendment traditions.

state_transitions.change: Summons Owen, identifies amendment traces, and warns that the castle may start completing half-made conditions on its own.
Dead before the dawn aftermath is fully processed by students.

characters.availability: Present through the occupation; dead by dawn on 2 May 1998.

characters.physical_details: Thickset, heavy-handed, quick to flush, and often smelling of sweat and drink.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Student target whose odd survivals increasingly irritate him.
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Punishment target and repeated public example.
key_relationships.neville_longbottom: Student enemy he never fully catches.
key_relationships.severus_snape: Superior he resents but nominally serves.

characters.occupation_status: Death Eater Dark Arts professor and chief student-level enforcer.
characters.literacy_languages: Ordinary magical literacy only; no understanding of the First Art.

state_transitions.change: Great Hall punishment reveals repeated contact distortions he cannot explain.
Takes Martha for exemplary punishment after the lower-west failure.
Loses power with the regime's collapse.

characters.availability: Present as an active threat until the battle; defeated afterward.

characters.physical_details: Small, soot-darkened, singe-eared house-elf with wet working hands; deep old-work spaces hurt him.

key_relationships.owen_phipps: Reluctant guide and later maintenance counterpart at the node.
key_relationships.martha_sorrell: Favored kitchen child who gets fed and spoken to plainly.
key_relationships.prue_hollis: Tolerated scholar who handles books and tools carefully.

characters.occupation_status: Kitchen and scullery house-elf with institutional memory of service routes, hush boxes, and maintenance marks.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads labels, inventories, and founder-era maintenance notation better than any student.

state_transitions.change: Explains the flour-bin aperture and hush-box logic that let Owen reach C-13.
Leads Owen into the East Undercroft despite the pain it causes him.
Brings battle-night repair materials to the threshold and reports the castle's upper-story misbehavior.
Helps the node take back the Leaves and instructs Owen in continuing maintenance intervals.

characters.availability: Present in kitchens and service runs throughout; cannot comfortably enter deep live-work zones but remains involved at their threshold.

timeline.story_start: Early September 1997, the first Hogwarts term under Snape after the Ministry falls to Voldemort.
timeline.estimated_span: About eight months, from the start of term to dawn on 2 May 1998.

seasonal_track.season: night of 1 May 1998 through dawn of 2 May 1998

key_events.event: Owen finds the North Stacks C-13 footnote in a 1878 Binns classroom copy of A History of Magic.
He reaches the retired shelf-chase through the kitchen-side aperture, opens the lead hush box, and takes the Grinlow Leaves.
Neville rejects Owen's warning, but Prue recognizes the citation as bibliographically impossible.
The greenhouse test proves the First Art is real and destabilizes Owen's wandwork.
Amycus's Great Hall punishment leaves a literal ward scar in the flagstones.
The East Undercroft confirms Hogwarts is one anchor in a planetary masking system built by the departed Meridian Commonwealth.
The lower-west corridor sabotage fails; Martha is punished and Neville bars Owen from further operations.
Owen and Prue map four remaining anchors and the Hogwarts-specific local branch.
The cracked star-lens shows that a major battle at Hogwarts would whiten the breach and attract outside predatory attention.
Harry returns on the night of 1 May 1998, the battle begins, and Owen goes below instead of joining the surface fight.
Owen repairs the seam, discovers and refuses the hostile-wandwork clause, and Prue witnesses the refusal before the battle turns without him.
key_events.timing: late night 1 May into early 2 May 1998

key_events.event: By dawn on 2 May 1998 the manuscript has been partly absorbed into the node and Owen signs for continued maintenance.

geography.primary_setting: Hogwarts under occupation, with ordinary school spaces above and founder-era infrastructure waking below.

key_locations.name: Hufflepuff cellar and common room
key_locations.details: Warm quarters near the kitchens; Owen's social baseline.

key_locations.name: Great Hall
key_locations.details: Public stage of occupation and later triage space; chapter 6 leaves a ward scar from the Hufflepuff bench toward the center aisle.

key_locations.name: Library north stacks and C-13 shelf-chase
key_locations.details: Blast-damaged bay exposing obsolete shelf numerals, the retired chase, and the lead hush box.

key_locations.name: Kitchens, scullery, and service routes
key_locations.details: Main labor space and main access to old infrastructure, including the flour-bin aperture behind the cracked blue tureen and the later third-rack and third-flue descent.

key_locations.name: Greenhouse row
key_locations.details: Abandoned house for Owen's first amendment and later winter workspace for him and Prue.

key_locations.name: Lower west corridor and service press
key_locations.details: Resistance extraction route damaged by Owen's threshold work; doors, cisterns, armor, and the press all answer as one passage system.

key_locations.name: East Undercroft
key_locations.details: Founder-era service chamber below the castle with the live Hogwarts anchor stone, floor rings, channels, and maintenance surfaces.

key_locations.name: Departure Chamber
key_locations.details: Lakeward transit chamber with waiting benches, old transport grooves, and the cracked star-lens that reads the ward skin from inside.

distances: The Hufflepuff cellar sits nearest the kitchens, letting Owen move between common room and service passages faster than most students.
The retired C-13 shelf-chase drops from the library north wall toward kitchen-side service space through an aperture too narrow for most adults.
The East Undercroft is reached from kitchen-side maintenance runs and, in battle conditions, by the third-flue descent beyond the scullery and coal bins.
The Departure Chamber lies farther down toward the lake and only opens as transit when the undercroft is made to answer that aspect.
The lower west corridor links the staff lavatory, classrooms, a fruit portrait, and service arches near the scullery.

world_rules: The story takes place during the 1997-1998 Hogwarts year under Snape, with the Carrows using classes, detentions, and public punishment as instruments of terror.
Ordinary Hogwarts movement rules still matter; people rely on corridors, portraits, staircases, and service passages rather than Apparition shortcuts.
The First Art is conditional amendment beneath spellcasting: it edits categories, thresholds, and allowed relations rather than producing a single obedient effect.
Live pages and tables answer only under the right conditions, including the coffer brand, astronomy overlays, an active node, or a ward scar strong enough to wake deeper infrastructure.
Amendments bind to fixed frames, thresholds, prepared surfaces, or founder stone; imprecise phrasing spreads across everything the chosen category includes.
Every successful amendment scars local wards and destabilizes nearby wandwork; under enough stress the castle can begin completing half-made conditions on its own terms.
House-elves understand service marks and old routes better than students, but deep live-work zones pain them physically.
The Meridian Commonwealth evacuated Earth deliberately after building a four-anchor masking ward; Hogwarts is one of the remaining anchors.
High magical discharge, especially battle-scale conflict at Hogwarts, brightens the breach and attracts predatory outside attention toward Earth.
The siege-state Hogwarts node can surface a founder clause that suspends hostile wandwork inside the castle, but it also suppresses defenders' hostile wandwork and competes directly with seam repair.

power_structure.mechanism: Political conquest backed by murder, occupation, and the Carrows' delegated violence.

power_structure.over: Students and remaining staff at Hogwarts
power_structure.mechanism: Formal headmaster authority, surveillance, and control of access under the occupying regime.

power_structure.over: Students in class, detention, and corridor discipline
power_structure.mechanism: Direct bodily violence, humiliation, and Dark Arts instruction.
Earned trust, operational planning, and willingness to take risks first.

power_structure.over: Kitchen labor, service routes, and old maintenance knowledge near the node
power_structure.mechanism: Institutional memory, daily access, and house-elf authority inside workspaces.

power_structure.over: Movement, doors, and wand relations inside the castle under extreme stress
power_structure.mechanism: Embedded house logic that can wake, reroute, or suppress conditions when old seams are activated.

power_structure.over: The live Hogwarts node by dawn after the battle
power_structure.mechanism: Direct claim through the coffer brand, battle-night repair, and his signed maintenance obligation.

objects.name: 1878 Binns classroom copy of A History of Magic
objects.origin: Old school text preserving the North Stacks C-13 footnote removed from later printings.
objects.status: Original lead to the Grinlow Leaves.

objects.name: The Grinlow Leaves
objects.owner: Owen Phipps until chapter 18, then the live node itself
objects.origin: Founder-era manuscript packet hidden in the C-13 lead hush box.
objects.status: Recovered in chapter 2 and partly absorbed into the undercroft stone after the battle, so no longer portable.

objects.name: Lead hush box from North Stacks C-13
objects.origin: Lead-lined coffer built to defeat cataloguing and Summoning magic.
objects.status: Forced open in chapter 2 and left damaged in the retired shelf-chase.

objects.name: Owen's ash-linen notebook
objects.origin: School notebook turned into the working copy for amendments, routes, tables, and battle-night notes.
objects.status: Survives, but some copied leaves are destroyed or pasted into the node seal in chapter 18.

objects.name: Hogwarts anchor stone
objects.owner: No personal owner; live component of the planetary mask under Hogwarts
objects.origin: Founder-era node embedded below the castle and tied to the larger Meridian ward system.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 7, partly repaired in chapter 16, and left requiring active maintenance in chapter 18.

objects.name: Departure Chamber star-lens
objects.owner: No personal owner; fixed monitoring instrument of the older system
objects.origin: Meridian transit infrastructure beneath the lake, built to read the ward skin from inside the mask.
objects.status: Activated in chapter 13; cracked but still legible enough to show the breach risk.

objects.name: Founder hostile-wandwork clause
objects.owner: Embedded in Hogwarts' local node logic rather than portable
objects.origin: Dormant house-protection branch that surfaces only under siege while a claimant is physically holding the live node.
objects.status: Discovered in chapter 16 and intentionally left unused while the battle is undecided.

knowledge_state.knows: The 1878 Binns copy preserves a real footnote to North Stacks C-13.
The Grinlow Leaves are real, answer to his branded palm, and describe First Art beneath ordinary magic.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All other characters until he lets Prue in.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen's citation is impossible in normal school printings and therefore points to deliberate removal.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most students until she proves it more fully in chapter 5.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen can alter the way punishment magic and force land, even when he cannot explain how.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Amycus, Alecto, and most other students as an explanation.

knowledge_state.knows: The East Undercroft contains a live anchor tied to a planetary masking system and departure infrastructure.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All other students and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: Four anchors remain active: Hogwarts, a red inland waste, a high western mountain spine, and a salt plateau in interior Asia; Hogwarts also carries a school-specific local branch.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Martha, Neville, Snape, and the Carrows.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen is practicing amendment magic serious enough to scar wards and wake autonomous responses in the castle, though Snape does not know the exact manuscript or site.
A major battle at Hogwarts will whiten the breach and attract predatory outside attention toward Earth.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone else unless Owen shares it.

knowledge_state.knows: The live node contains a founder clause that could suspend hostile wandwork across Hogwarts during the battle.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone else until Prue reaches the chamber.

knowledge_state.knows: Owen had a live way to suspend hostile wandwork while the battle was undecided and consciously refused it.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most survivors, including Martha and Neville, unless Prue later chooses to speak.

knowledge_state.knows: After the battle the Leaves are effectively part of the node and the seam now requires first-light and last-light checks for seep, brightness, load, and travel.

environmental_constants: The castle is cold in a way that finds wrists, ankles, and knees even when fires are lit.
Occupied Hogwarts smells of soot, old paper, damp wool, and institutional food trying to be comforting.
The kitchens are warmer, wetter, and louder than the upper school, grounding quiet scenes in labor, bread, and practical care.
The library's damaged north side keeps a grit of dust and splintered wood after the blast that exposed the old shelf numerals.
First Art sites feel wrong in the body before explanation: tooth-pressure, hot stone, ringing ears, nausea, thickened air, and sound traveling through bone.
The undercroft and lakeward chambers are damp and mineral, with voices reduced to thuds and pressure through stone.
During the battle, smoke and stone dust reach even the subterranean scenes.
